DECLARATION OF ROSS LONG IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS. TO PLTF'S MTN TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT LONG, ROSS, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS TOBY LONG, AIA, DBA TOBY LONG DESIGN

PLAINTIFFS DAVID AND CATHLEEN HAYES MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROVISIONS OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1284.3(A). IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 1284.3(A) DOES NOT AUTHORIZE THE COURT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, MERELY PROHIBIT IT WHERE THE CLAIMS ARE ILLEGAL OR UNCONSCIONABLE. THE PROVISION IN QUESTION HERE IS NEITHER ILLEGAL NOR UNCONSCIONABLE. MOREOVER, THE COURT IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO "RE-WRITE" THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT UNDER CCP SECTION 1284.3(A) IN ORDER TO COMPEL THE ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797 (302)
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-31-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT LONG, ROSS, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS TOBY LONG, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is off calendar. A First Amended Complaint was filed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel Arbitration In Compliance With The Provisions Of Ccp Sec 1284.3(A)
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFFS DAVID AND CATHLEEN HAYES MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE PROVISIONS OF CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1284.3(a).

APPEARANCES: Terence O. Mayo, Esq. of Mayo & Mayo 397-1515 for pltfs & moving parties; Daniel P. Iannitelli, Esq. of Fotouhi Epps Hillger Gilroy 362-9300 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: (DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL brought IN CHECK TO PAY FILING FEE as requested in the tentative ruling.) Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Motion to compel is denied. CCP section 1284.3(a) does not authorize the Court to compel arbitration, merely prohibit it where the claims are illegal or unconscionable. The provision in question here is neither illegal nor unconscionable. Moreover, the Court is not authorized to "re-write" the arbitration agreement under CCP section 1284.3(a) in order to compel the arbitration. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
